STRAIT OF HORMUZ TRAFFIC SURGES AS SHIPPING CONFIDENCE RETURNS

VESSEL TRAFFIC THROUGH THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ HAS REBOUNDED SHARPLY, WITH CONFIRMED CROSSINGS NEARLY TRIPLING WEEK-ON-WEEK, SIGNALING IMPROVING CONFIDENCE AMONG SHIP OPERATORS AFTER RECENT GEOPOLITICAL TENSIONS.

ACCORDING TO MARINETRAFFIC AND KPLER DATA, VESSEL CROSSINGS ROSE FROM 32 BETWEEN JUNE 12–14 TO 93 BETWEEN JUNE 19–21, AN INCREASE OF 61 CROSSINGS. THE BIGGEST JUMP OCCURRED ON SATURDAY, WHEN CROSSINGS SURGED FROM JUST 3 VESSELS TO 42 COMPARED WITH THE PREVIOUS WEEKEND.

THE RECOVERY APPEARS TO BE DRIVEN BY EASING CONCERNS OVER REGIONAL DISRUPTIONS, RECENT DIPLOMATIC DEVELOPMENTS, AND A TEMPORARY OFAC GENERAL LICENSE THAT REDUCED COMPLIANCE UNCERTAINTY FOR APPROVED HORMUZ TRANSITS THROUGH AUGUST 21. ...
